seo.title: 100 SvelteKit resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical SvelteKit resources for engineers.

content.intro: This resource guide provides technical implementations for SvelteKit developers focusing on Svelte 5 runes, edge deployment strategies, and high-performance data patterns using modern adapters and ORMs.

items.title: @sveltejs/adapter-cloudflare
items.description: Configure wrangler.toml to expose KV namespaces and D1 databases to the platform-specific 'platform' object in SvelteKit load functions.

items.title: Vercel ISR Implementation
items.description: Utilize config.incrementalStaticRegeneration in +page.server.ts to cache dynamic routes at the edge for specific durations.

items.title: adapter-node Cluster Mode
items.description: Deploy SvelteKit on multi-core VPS instances using PM2 to manage multiple node processes for increased request throughput.

items.title: Dynamic Environment Variables
items.description: Use $env/dynamic/private for runtime configuration like API keys that must not be baked into the build artifact for security compliance.

items.title: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) Hooks
items.description: Implement a handle hook in hooks.server.ts to inject Access-Control-Allow-Origin headers for API-only SvelteKit routes.

items.title: Cloudflare Workers Local Simulation
items.description: Run 'wrangler pages dev' with the --compatibility-date flag to mirror the production edge environment during local development.

items.title: Prerendering Specific Routes
items.description: Set 'export const prerender = true' in +layout.ts for static documentation or marketing sub-trees to reduce server load.

items.title: Custom Error Handling
items.description: Create a +error.svelte file to capture and format errors from load functions without exposing sensitive stack traces to users.

items.title: Vercel Cron Jobs
items.description: Define scheduled tasks in vercel.json that target specific SvelteKit API routes for automated database maintenance or report generation.

items.title: Streaming with adapter-auto
items.description: Enable streaming responses in your host platform settings to allow SvelteKit load functions to pipe data chunks to the client.

items.title: $state for Reactive Declarations
items.description: Replace let declarations with the $state() rune to opt-into fine-grained reactivity in Svelte 5 components and logic files.

items.title: $derived for Computed Values
items.description: Use $derived() to create reactive values that automatically update when their dependencies change, replacing the legacy $: syntax.

items.title: $effect for Side Effects
items.description: Implement $effect() to manage DOM manipulations or external API synchronizations, ensuring proper cleanup through returned functions.

items.title: $props with Snippets
items.description: Define component interfaces using the $props() rune and utilize snippets to pass UI blocks as arguments for better code reuse.

items.title: $bindable for Two-Way Binding
items.description: Explicitly mark component props as $bindable() to allow parent components to synchronize state back from child components.

items.title: npx sv migrate
items.description: Execute the official migration script to automatically convert Svelte 4 syntax and stores into Svelte 5 rune patterns.

items.title: Class-based Reactive Logic
items.description: Encapsulate complex state logic inside JavaScript classes using runes to create portable, reactive controllers outside of .svelte files.

items.title: $inspect for Debugging
items.description: Inject the $inspect() rune into your code to log reactive state changes to the console without manual console.log triggers.

items.title: Typed Snippets for TypeScript
items.description: Define Snippet types in Svelte 5 to ensure that UI fragments passed as props adhere to strict data structures.

items.title: Untracking State
items.description: Use the untrack() function within an effect to read a reactive value without adding it to the effect's dependency list.

items.title: SvelteKit Form Actions
items.description: Use +page.server.ts actions to handle POST requests, providing automatic CSRF protection and progressive enhancement capabilities.

items.title: AI Streaming with ReadableStream
items.description: Implement a +server.ts endpoint that returns a ReadableStream from OpenAI or Anthropic for real-time text generation in the UI.

items.title: Drizzle ORM Integration
items.description: Configure Drizzle with SvelteKit server routes to perform type-safe SQL queries against PostgreSQL or SQLite databases.

items.title: Lucia Auth Session Management
items.description: Integrate Lucia in hooks.server.ts to validate session cookies and populate event.locals.user for access control across routes.

items.title: Superforms for Validation
items.description: Use the sveltekit-superforms library with Zod schemas to handle server-side validation and client-side error display.

items.title: Parallel Load Functions
items.description: Avoid awaiting parent() data at the start of load functions to allow SvelteKit to fetch layout and page data concurrently.

items.title: Stripe Checkout Sessions
items.description: Create server actions that initialize Stripe Checkout sessions and redirect users to the hosted payment page securely.

items.title: PocketBase JS SDK Setup
items.description: Initialize the PocketBase client in hooks.server.ts to maintain authentication state between the browser and the server.

items.title: Cache-Control Header Injection
items.description: Set 'setHeaders' in load functions to control browser and CDN caching behavior for dynamic content.

items.title: Optimistic UI Updates
items.description: Use the applyAction helper in use:enhance to update the UI immediately before the server round-trip completes.

pro_tips: Use data-sveltekit-preload-data='hover' on anchor tags to reduce perceived latency by fetching page data when a user hovers over a link.
Implement the handle error hook in hooks.server.ts to pipe server-side exceptions directly to Sentry or Axiom for production monitoring.
Leverage the snapshot feature in +page.svelte to preserve scroll position and form input values when a user navigates away and back.
Use $app/paths 'base' and 'assets' imports to ensure your application works correctly when deployed to a sub-path or a custom CDN.
Avoid putting large objects in $page.data; instead, use individual API endpoints for heavy data to keep the initial HTML payload small.
